summary: Jeff Atwood argues that 2014 marks a turning point where tablet performance has nearly caught up to desktop computers, validating his bet that the mobile web browser will become the dominant computing platform. He presents benchmark data showing the iPad Air 2 performing JavaScript within striking distance of his desktop machine. While praising Apple's shift to performance leadership and Android 5's aesthetic improvements, he notes Android's continued erratic JavaScript performance issues. The post serves as both a device review and a broader argument that the 'all computers will be tablets' future is arriving faster than expected.
key_points: Tablet JavaScript performance is now within striking distance of desktop performance, with iPad Air 2 scoring 225ms vs desktop's 180ms
The mobile web browser represents the path of least resistance that will capture computing's 'obscene amount of superpower'
Apple has shifted from aesthetic premium to raw performance leadership
Android 5.0 is aesthetically superior to iOS 8 but still suffers from erratic performance, especially in Chrome's V8 engine
The Surface Pro 3 serves as a preview of what iPad performance will look like in 2-3 years
money_quotes: One of my big bets with Discourse is that eventually, all computers will be tablets of varying size, with performance basically indistinguishable from a two year old desktop or laptop.
Apps are great and all, but there has to be some place for this year's bumper crop of obscene amount of computing superpower to go.
It's hard to believe we now live in a world where the Apple 'Premium' is no longer about aesthetics, but raw, unbridled, class-leading performance.

I patiently explain that it's a very nice third gen hardware design with a fully integrated keyboard cover, IE11 is a great touch browser, and that I'm mostly using the device as a tablet, as a sneak preview of what iPad 8 performance will look like.
Performance-wise, it is what I've come to expect from Android: erratic.
Remember how people in the year 2000 used to say how crazy and ridiculous it was, the idea that Anyone Would Ever Run Photoshop in a Web Browser? I mean come on.
key_insight: The convergence of tablet and desktop performance validates betting on the mobile web browser as the universal computing platform of the future.
